event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with Peppa Pig and her family walking down a vibrant street, passing shops and a bakery, as upbeat music plays. A Panda Police Officer and a Mail Carrier are seen, and Peppa excitedly announces a "big party tonight!" The Pig family—Daddy Pig, Mummy Pig, Peppa, and George—are on the sidewalk, showing a smartphone photo to Mr. Bull, who inquires about the occasion. Peppa reveals they have "really big news" to share, prompting Daddy Pig to nervously deflect. George questions Mr. Bull's comment about wearing bells, and Mummy Pig reminds them to keep the news secret until they tell Granny and Grandpa Pig. As they continue, Peppa worries about being asked, and Daddy Pig advises talking about something else, which George confidently agrees to. They encounter Mr. Dog with balloons and Madame Gazelle, both showing family photos and asking about the celebration. The scene then shifts to an indoor party with balloons and streamers, featuring a chocolate cake with a toy digger. Mummy Pig and Daddy Pig express relief at keeping the secret. The party guests, including Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, and Madame Gazelle, arrive, wearing party hats. Madame Gazelle expresses excitement for a "big hat party," and Mr. Dog congratulates them on a "new bakery." The final segment reveals the "really big news": Mummy Pig is pregnant. Peppa excitedly announces, "Mum, Dad, we're having a baby!" and declares she'll be an "even bigger sister." The party culminates with dancing, cheers, and celebratory sounds as everyone celebrates the upcoming baby.

He is seen smiling and hugging Mummy Pig during the party.

She speaks with a distinct female voice, suggesting 'different news,' agreeing to turn down the music ('Okay'), excitedly announcing 'Mum, Dad, we're having a baby!', and declaring she'll be an 'even bigger sister.' She is described as 'most excited of all.'

Speaker.description: A large black speaker with a circular grille, placed near the music player, contributing to the party’s sound system.

raw_segments: The video opens on a vibrant street scene, featuring various shops like a teapot shop and a fruit store, as cheerful, upbeat background music begins. A Panda Police Officer stands beside a black car, holding a yellow flower, while a Mail Carrier walks past. A male narrator announces, "Today, Peppa and her family are having a..." before Peppa Pig excitedly finishes, "...big party tonight!" The scene shifts to a close-up of a smartphone screen displaying a photo of Peppa Pig and her family with a pink, floral background. The camera pulls back to show the Pig family—Daddy Pig, Mummy Pig, Peppa, and George—standing on the sidewalk. Daddy Pig, with his deep, booming voice, exclaims, "See you there!", followed by Mummy Pig's gentle voice, "It's gonna be fun!" They are greeted by Mr. Bull, a brown bull in an orange shirt, who holds up his phone showing the same family photo and gruffly asks, "A party? What's the occasion?" The text 'Really Big News' appears above them. Peppa, with her excited child's voice, reveals, "Oh, it's because we have to tell everyone the really big news!" Mummy Pig gasps, and Daddy Pig nervously tries to cover, "Just a party for... reasons." Mr. Bull laughs heartily, declaring, "Oh, lovely! I'll be there with bells on!" George Pig, with a curious child's voice, asks, "Why would he wear bells?" Mummy Pig then quietly reminds them, "Remember, we're not telling anyone the really big news until we've told Granny and Grandpa Pig at the party." As the group walks down the street, passing a bakery with cakes in the window, Peppa, concerned, asks, "But what if they ask us?" Daddy Pig calmly advises, "If someone asks, see if you can talk about something else." George makes a squealing sound and confidently states, "I can do that!" The narrator explains that Peppa and George are helping to prepare for the party and doing their best not to tell everyone the really big news. Peppa gasps again. They encounter Mr. Dog, a gray dog in a red cap, holding balloons shaped like a dinosaur and a bear, and showing a family photo on his phone, asking in a friendly, slightly confused voice, "Ooh, what are we celebrating?" The narrator notes that everyone is very interested. Finally, Madame Gazelle, a yellow gazelle in a chef’s hat, shows them a cake-themed family photo on her phone, starting to say in an elegant, slightly curious voice, "Ooh, big..." as the segment ends, maintaining a cheerful and celebratory atmosphere.

The minute opens with Peppa Pig, George Pig, Mummy Pig, and Daddy Pig walking cheerfully through a vibrant town street, passing a bakery displaying cakes and a market. An unidentified female voice asks, "News, is it?" as Mr. Dog joins them, holding dinosaur and bear balloons, followed by Mr. Bull, whose arrival is marked by a car horn sound, urging, "Come on, little piggies!" He shows a smartphone with a Pig family photo. Madame Gazelle, in her chef’s hat, also appears, holding a phone with a cake-themed photo. Peppa Pig, with her high-pitched voice, excitedly guesses the occasion, first as a "baking shop," then a "big hat party," and finally a "baby carrot," which George Pig, in his small voice, echoes as "lots of baby carrots." Daddy Pig, in his booming voice, exclaims "Oh! Wow!" in surprise. Mummy Pig, with a warm tone, playfully asks George, "Really, George? Wee!" The scene transitions to a festive indoor birthday celebration, adorned with colorful balloons and streamers. A large chocolate cake with a toy digger sits on a table. Mummy Pig, now in a pink dress and pearl necklace, beams, while Daddy Pig, in a teal suit, looks surprised then delighted. The narrator announces it's "time for the party and almost time for the really big news." Mummy Pig expresses satisfaction, "Well done, team. We managed to keep the secret for the whole day," to which Daddy Pig adds, "I wasn't sure we'd make it." Peppa Pig confirms, "Yep, we didn't tell anyone the real news, did we, George?" prompting Daddy Pig to question, "Wait, what do you mean, real news?" The celebration expands to include Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, and Madame Gazelle, all wearing party hats and posing for a group photo. Madame Gazelle, with an enthusiastic voice, declares she's "Excited for the big hat party!" and Mr. Dog, in a gruff voice, congratulates them on a "new bakery!" The atmosphere remains joyful and vibrant, with everyone smiling.

The video opens with a close-up of George Pig, in a blue shirt with a yellow star, excitedly jumping beside Peppa Pig, in a red dress, as they stand near a birthday cake topped with a toy digger. A male voice, likely Daddy Pig, asks, "You know about the competition though?" to which a female voice, Peppa, suggests, "maybe we told people different news instead." The scene quickly expands to reveal a lively party room filled with colorful balloons and festive decorations. Daddy Pig, now in a teal suit, exclaims, "Gosh, what's all this then?" as upbeat, catchy, pop-like music with a strong beat begins, featuring multiple voices singing playful, rhyming lyrics about "news," "clues," "shoes," and "tattoos." Daddy Pig and Mummy Pig, in a pink dress, are seen smiling and interacting with guests, including Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, and Madame Gazelle, who are holding smartphones displaying photos of the Pig family. The group gathers around the cake, and Daddy Pig and Mummy Pig share a joyful moment, laughing and hugging. The camera then focuses on a large speaker and a colorful cube-shaped music player, which Peppa Pig approaches with delight. Daddy Pig asks, "Can you turn the music down please, Peppa?" and Peppa responds, "Okay," as the music volume slightly lowers. The music then shifts to a celebratory chorus singing, "We're having a baby!" followed by a collective "Ooh!" Peppa excitedly announces, "Mum, Dad, we're having a baby!" The narrator confirms, "Everyone's very excited for the new baby," and Peppa declares, "I'm going to be an even bigger sister!" with the narrator adding, "But Peppa is most excited of all." The party reaches its peak as the room fills with balloons, and the characters dance and celebrate together, with Peppa Pig and George Pig jumping joyfully in the center amidst cheers and celebratory sounds like "Woohoo!" and "Bravo!", all wearing party hats and smiling under the colorful decorations.

event_blocks.summary: Mummy Pig is initially sleeping, with Daddy Pig narrating her lie-in, punctuated by snores and yawns, until the quiet is broken by excited squeals and a party blower, signaling a surprise celebration with colorful balloons and decorations. Daddy Pig, Peppa Pig, and George Pig present Mummy Pig with a birthday cake, shouting "Happy Mummy's Day!" Mummy Pig awakens happily, thanking them and marveling at the "so many balloons," to which Daddy Pig humorously describes them as "eco-friendly" and "loud." Peppa explains the occasion is "Even More Mummy's Day" because of the "baby in your tummy," a sentiment the family echoes. Peppa presents the two-layered cake with a single candle to Mummy Pig, who, amused, questions "Cake for breakfast?" before accepting and suggesting they move downstairs. As they descend the staircase, Daddy Pig notes the hallway is also filled with balloons, prompting Mummy Pig's delighted exclamation, "Oh, even more balloons!" Peppa confirms, "For Even More Mummy's Day!" Mummy Pig humorously remarks, "You know, sometimes, less is more!" amidst the continued excited squeals of Peppa and George, maintaining a cheerful atmosphere.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Mummy Pig peacefully sleeping, her gentle snores and yawns filling the quiet room. Daddy Pig's calm, deep voice narrates, explaining that Mummy Pig is enjoying a relaxing lie-in. Suddenly, the tranquility is shattered by excited squeals and a party blower as the room bursts into a vibrant celebration, filled with colorful balloons and festive decorations. Daddy Pig, Peppa Pig, and George Pig joyfully surprise Mummy Pig with a birthday cake, all shouting, "Happy Mummy's Day!" Mummy Pig wakes with delight, her voice surprised and happy as she thanks them and exclaims, "Oh, so many balloons!" Daddy Pig chuckles, playfully adding that the balloons are "eco-friendly" and "loud." Peppa, in her child-like, enthusiastic voice, explains the special occasion: "Now there's a baby in your tummy, we thought you're going to be even more of a mummy, so today is..." The family then choruses, "Even More Mummy's Day!" as the title text appears on screen. Peppa, holding the two-layered cake with a single candle, presents it to Mummy Pig, who, amused, asks, "Cake for breakfast?" before accepting and suggesting they go downstairs. Daddy Pig teasingly asks if she's truly awake, to which Mummy Pig playfully replies, "I am now!" As the family descends the staircase, still surrounded by balloons, Daddy Pig narrates that the hallway is also filled. Mummy Pig, delighted, exclaims, "Oh, even more balloons!" Peppa reiterates, "For Even More Mummy's Day!" Mummy Pig chuckles, making an ironic comment, "You know, sometimes, less is more!" amidst the continued excited squeals from Peppa and George, maintaining a cheerful and loving atmosphere throughout.

Daddy Pig, standing beside Mummy Pig, notices a daisy chain leading from the living room, which Peppa reveals they made together. In the living room, adorned with daisy garlands, the family gathers around the cake on a coffee table. Peppa proudly states their effort in making the daisy chain, and Daddy Pig expresses his admiration. Peppa presents more cake, emphasizing it's Mummy Pig's day and requires even more daisies, to which George grunts in agreement, causing Daddy Pig to hesitate. The scene transitions to a sunny meadow where Daddy Pig and the children interact with daisies before returning indoors. Later, in the kitchen, Mummy Pig is in a pink bed as Peppa and George present the cake. Mummy Pig, waking up, delightedly exclaims thanks for the cake, humorously acknowledging she's eating for two. Daddy Pig begins a funny story as Peppa demands more, and the video concludes with Peppa and George smiling in front of a refrigerator displaying a family photo, highlighting a warm, celebratory, and family-centered mood.

raw_segments: The video opens with the Pig family celebrating a joyful occasion in their home, decorated with colorful balloons and festive garlands. Peppa Pig, in her red dress, excitedly exclaims "Ah! Go!" as she jumps around with George, holding a two-layered birthday cake with pink frosting and a single candle. Daddy Pig, in his teal suit and glasses, asks "Ooh, what's this coming from the living room?" as he stands beside Mummy Pig, who is in her pink dress. Peppa excitedly reveals, "Oh, a daisy chain!" and instructs, "You have to follow it." Daddy Pig expresses "Oh, wow!" as the scene transitions to the living room, now adorned with daisy garlands, where the family gathers around a coffee table with the cake. Peppa proudly states, "It took us ages to make," to which Daddy Pig replies, "I can only imagine." Peppa then presents "Here's more cake!" and explains, "We all made the daisy chain together." Daddy Pig gently tells George, "That's enough daisies now, George. Sometimes, less is more," but Peppa playfully counters, "It's even more Mummy Pig's day, so we need even more daisies, George!" George makes grunting sounds in agreement with Peppa, prompting Daddy Pig to respond with a hesitant "Um..." The setting then shifts outdoors to a sunny meadow filled with daisies, where Daddy Pig and the children playfully interact with the flowers, before returning indoors to the living room, still celebrating with the cake. The final scenes show the family in the kitchen, where Mummy Pig is lying in a pink bed. Peppa triumphantly announces "Ta-da!" as she and George present the cake to her. Daddy Pig laughs, and Mummy Pig, waking up, delightedly exclaims, "Oh, cake! Thanks, George." She humorously adds, "I know I'm eating for two, but..." and playfully guesses, "Oh! Let me guess. Thought so." Daddy Pig begins to tell a "Funny story about this..." as Peppa, still excited, demands "More!" The video ends with Peppa and George smiling happily in front of a refrigerator displaying a family photo, encapsulating a warm, celebratory, and family-centered mood focused on love and togetherness.

event_blocks.summary: At a bakery shop, amidst car engine sounds and a 'ding', Daddy Pig uses a pink smartphone to select a layered cake from a digital menu, tapping the screen and remarking, "Oh, just a little bigger," with his choice confirmed by a green checkmark. Peppa Pig exclaims, "Woo! Yay! Whoa, that's enough!" and George Pig adds excited squeals. The family then drives home in their red car, the cake secured on the roof, as Daddy Pig states, "Whoa, that's enough, George." A whooshing sound and another 'ding' mark their arrival at a brightly lit kitchen where Mummy Pig is present. Gathered around a table with multiple cakes, Daddy Pig shows his smartphone displaying a family photo, saying, "Oh, not that picture," as an automated voice confirms, "Order confirmed." Peppa Pig shouts, "Yay! Hooray! Woohoo! Yay!" with George Pig adding more squeals and declaring, "Even more cake! Even more Mummy's Day!" Mummy Pig laughs softly, thanking them and suggesting, "We can share it with everyone we know!" A 'ding' accompanies a transition to the family in front of a large, colorful family drawing adorned with hearts and stripes. Mummy Pig inquires, "Is that what I think it is?" and George Pig excitedly shouts, "Happy even more Mummy's Day!" Daddy Pig chuckles, and Mummy Pig exclaims, "What a lovely, huge card!" Daddy Pig observes, "Mummy Pig knows that less is more," but Mummy Pig adds, "One thing is still missing though." Daddy Pig replies, "But there is one more thing she'd like," prompting Peppa Pig to excitedly shout, "A family squeeze!" The family then shares a warm hug, with Daddy Pig and Mummy Pig embracing, while Peppa and George stand beside them, smiling, accompanied by excited squeals, laughter, and Daddy Pig's chuckles, culminating in a joyful and celebratory atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Mummy Pig experiences cravings, initially depicted in a dreamlike sequence with swirling food items and light music, before transitioning to a lively party where she explains to Daddy Pig, Peppa Pig, and George Pig that her food preferences are "always changing, and sometimes a bit strange" due to her pregnancy, recalling past cravings for ketchup and bananas with George, and strawberries and mayonnaise with Peppa. Peppa suggests using a map to help Mummy Pig choose, but the map dramatically breaks, prompting Daddy Pig to promise a quick fix. The narrative then shifts to a brightly lit restaurant with a checkered floor where the family gathers around a colorful Spinning Wheel; Peppa Pig enthusiastically suggests using it to pick food, and after Daddy Pig spins it to land on salad, which he finds less appealing than pasta, Mummy Pig spins it to land on noodles, expressing delight. As they move to a table where Mr. Bull serves noodles, Mummy Pig decides she doesn't want noodles after all, finding them "a bit less wiggly." The wheel is spun again, landing on fish, which Mummy Pig rejects, and finally on pizza, which Peppa declares everyone loves, though Mummy Pig expresses doubt and Daddy Pig admits "Sorry, cravings." Madame Gazelle then serves them pizza.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a whimsical, abstract sequence featuring Mummy Pig in an orange dress, floating amidst colorful, swirling backgrounds with floating food items like soup and cake, suggesting a dreamlike state of cravings, accompanied by light, dreamy music. A voiceover announces, "Mummy Pig is having cravings," as the word 'Cravings' appears in bold red text. The scene transitions to a lively indoor party setting where Mummy Pig, now smiling, stands beside Daddy Pig, Peppa Pig, and George Pig. Daddy Pig, wearing glasses and a teal suit, greets Mummy Pig, who then explains, "I can't decide what to eat. My cravings keep changing." Peppa Pig, in a red dress, asks, "What's cravings?" Mummy Pig clarifies that having a baby means her food preferences are "always changing, and sometimes a bit strange." Daddy Pig recalls, "with George, you always ate ketchup and bananas," prompting a disgusted "Ew!" from George Pig. Mummy Pig retorts that when Peppa was in her tummy, she "craved strawberries and mayonnaise," eliciting a collective "Yuck!" from the children. Mummy Pig then confidently states, "It's all right. I'll figure it out." Peppa suggests, "We can use this map to help you choose, Mummy!" to which Mummy Pig responds, "Oh, good idea!" Peppa excitedly points out options like "pizza, or noodles, or whoa!" before the map dramatically breaks. Daddy Pig calmly remarks, "Oh dear, the map has broken," but quickly reassures, "No worries, I'll fix that in a jiff!" The scene concludes with a voiceover mentioning that this event has given Peppa an idea, as the family stands near food tables with other characters like a rabbit, a brown bear, and a gazelle, all in a bright, cheerful atmosphere.

The video opens in a brightly lit restaurant with checkered flooring, where Daddy Pig, Mummy Pig, Peppa Pig, and George Pig gather around a large, colorful Spinning Wheel. Daddy Pig, with his deep voice, initially suggests "A great idea," but Peppa Pig, in her high-pitched, enthusiastic voice, clarifies, "Wait, we can still use it to pick the food, like a spinny wheel!" As Daddy Pig spins the wheel, which features various food icons, Peppa exclaims "Whee!" Daddy Pig then says, "Oh, I see. Let's have a go. I'll have..." The wheel spins with a distinct whirring sound and stops on salad, prompting Peppa to shout "Tada!" Daddy Pig, however, expresses mild disappointment, noting, "Oh, salad. That's uh, just as good as a big bowl of pasta." Peppa then encourages, "Your turn, Mummy! Whee!" The wheel spins again, landing on noodles. Mummy Pig, with a warm voice, excitedly declares, "Noodles! I love noodles! Let's go!" The family moves to a table where Mr. Bull, holding a bowl of noodles, cheerfully exclaims, "Mmm, noodles for lunch!" as they all enjoy their meal. Peppa describes the noodles as "so wiggly," to which Mummy Pig agrees, "Yes, they are," but then changes her mind, "Maybe not noodles. Let's try again. Maybe something a bit less wiggly." The scene shifts back to the wheel, which Daddy Pig spins again. Peppa identifies "Fish!" when it lands there, but Mummy Pig quickly rejects it with "Ooh, no." Finally, the wheel lands on pizza, and Peppa reacts with joy, confidently stating, "Everybody loves pizza, Mummy!" Mummy Pig expresses doubt, "Maybe not everybody, Peppa," while Daddy Pig, with a hint of longing, admits, "Sorry, cravings." Mummy Pig playfully responds, "Oh, you!" The family then moves to a counter where Madame Gazelle serves them pizza, maintaining the cheerful and playful atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: The Pig family arrived at a restaurant characterized by checkered flooring and purple walls, where Daddy Pig spun a food-themed wheel. Mummy Pig experienced a minor mishap, apologizing, "Excuse me, sorry," as Daddy Pig navigated the space, announcing, "Coming through!" and then exclaiming, "Oh!" Peppa inquired about Mummy Pig's well-being, who reassured her and suggested she would try again later, prompting Daddy Pig to encourage the children, "Come on, team. You heard Mummy." While Daddy Pig narrated Mummy Pig's uncertainty about finding food, she noticed a pickle, remarking, "Ooh, that pickle smells good, George," and asked Peppa to borrow some. Daddy Pig questioned the need for all the bread and told Mummy Pig, "Help yourself, pickle." Mummy Pig then successfully created and savored a "peanut butter and pickle sandwich," declaring, "Mmm, perfect." The family, including Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, Madame Gazelle, and a Rabbit, shared a joyful meal, laughing and enjoying their food in the restaurant's cheerful atmosphere.

Peppa Pig, in a red dress, questions, "Are you sure?" but Mummy Pig enthusiastically confirms, "Oh yes. Broccoli pasta is delish." The wheel, however, lands on salad with a distinct stopping sound, prompting Peppa to exclaim, "Uh-oh." Mummy Pig, initially delighted, then has a minor mishap, apologizing, "Excuse me, sorry." Mummy Pig reassures her, "Fine, darling, thank you," and suggests, "Why don't you all get your lunch and I'll try again in a little while." Daddy Pig encourages the children, "Come on, team. You heard Mummy." Peppa excitedly shouts, "Yay!" while George makes a sound of surprise. Daddy Pig narrates Mummy Pig's struggle, saying, "But Mummy's not sure she'll ever find something to eat." Mummy Pig then notices something, remarking, "Ooh, that pickle smells good, George," and asks Peppa, "And uh, could I borrow a bit of this, Peppa?" Peppa agrees with an "Okay!" Daddy Pig, observing, comments, "Don't need all this bread, do you?" and affectionately tells Mummy Pig, "Help yourself, pickle." Daddy Pig narrates Mummy Pig's success, "Mummy Pig has finally found something to eat," describing it as "A peanut butter and pickle sandwich." Mummy Pig triumphantly presents her creation with a "Tada!" and savors it, murmuring, "Mmm, perfect." Daddy Pig concludes, "It's your new..." as the segment ends, implying a new favorite. The family shares a joyful meal, laughing and enjoying their food amidst other patrons like Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, Madame Gazelle, and a Rabbit, adding to the cheerful atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: In a brightly lit classroom, Mr. Bull slurps noodles while Peppa Pig sits with cake, but the scene shifts to a zebra character struggling with two crying baby zebras in a stroller, lamenting their fussiness until Daddy Pig confidently asserts his 'magic touch.' Peppa then joyfully plays a drum and xylophone as the family gathers for a 'Baby Check' announcement, revealing Mummy Pig is the one receiving a check-up. Mummy Pig expresses the reality of impending motherhood, and Peppa reassures her, echoing Daddy Pig's confidence. The setting transitions to a medical room where Mummy Pig, in a hospital gown, stands with a White Rabbit Doctor, while Peppa and George Pig observe. Peppa eagerly anticipates a 'baby check together,' and Mummy Pig feels better. The White Rabbit Doctor, holding a red medical box, welcomes them for the baby check, explaining the need for tests. Peppa mistakenly calls the doctor 'Doctor Polar Bear' and offers her own heartbeat first, which the doctor confirms is healthy, along with Mummy Pig's. A tape is used to measure Mummy Pig's bump. Mummy Pig expresses a need for a toilet break, and the doctor provides a container. George Pig interrupts by blowing a horn, causing Peppa to laugh. After Mummy Pig returns, the doctor prepares to listen further. Later, in the medical room, Mummy Pig lies in bed as the White Rabbit Doctor explains the Doppler machine allows them to hear the baby. Daddy Pig smiles, and Peppa excitedly expresses her eagerness to meet the baby and asks numerous questions. George Pig makes babbling sounds. The doctor reassures them the baby cannot talk yet but will soon hear a special sound. A clear, rhythmic baby heartbeat is heard, delighting Daddy Pig and Peppa, who confirms it's the baby's heart. The doctor confirms all sounds are perfect. Peppa expresses wonder about the baby and innocent worries about the baby's preferences, which the doctor gently dismisses, emphasizing a routine baby check-up focused on family bonding and anticipation.

The scene unfolds in a brightly lit medical room with light green walls, a blue filing cabinet, a desk with a purple phone, and a framed certificate, all set to a gentle, cheerful background music. Peppa excitedly exclaims, "Yes!" after a small "whoops" sound, and the doctor responds, "Lovely," before explaining she needs to do "some quick tests to make sure you and the baby are healthy." The doctor then states she'll "need to listen to your heartbeat, Mummy Pig." Peppa, with her childlike voice, eagerly offers, "You can listen to my heartbeat first, Doctor Polar Bear!" misidentifying the doctor, and adding, "That way Mummy will know it's A-OK." The doctor gently responds, "Oh, Peppa, that's very sweet," as Peppa laughs. A stethoscope sound of a heartbeat is heard, followed by the doctor declaring, "Perfect. A clear, rhythmic baby's heartbeat is heard, prompting a delighted "Oh!" from Daddy Pig. Peppa, amazed, asks, "Is that the baby's heart?" The cheerful and affectionate atmosphere, underscored by gentle, whimsical background music, emphasizes a routine baby check-up focused on family bonding and the anticipation of the new arrival.

event_blocks.summary: In a medical room with light green walls, the White Rabbit Doctor measures Mummy Pig, who is in a pink bed, with a purple medical device, while a male narrator reassures that "everything is going to be A-OK" and "Peppa's very excited to meet the new baby." Daddy Pig smiles, and Peppa and George Pig watch; George honks a yellow horn, and the narrator adds, "the baby can't wait to meet her either." Peppa speculates it's a "baby's brother," George corrects to "or a sister," and Peppa playfully suggests "or a... dinosaur!" which George agrees to before Peppa retracts. The narrator clarifies that "Peppa and George are going to find out if the baby in Mummy Pig's tummy is a little brother or sister." George makes a happy sound, and Daddy Pig reassures, "It doesn't matter what the baby is," while Mummy Pig adds, "Just as long as it's happy and healthy." The scene shifts to George's drawing of a pink cat with a blue belly, which Peppa shows to Daddy Pig. Outside, in a sunny yard before a yellow house, Granny Pig and Grandpa Pig react to the drawings. Peppa asks if the "baby balloon" color indicates the baby's gender, and Daddy Pig confirms but warns not to pop or let it "float away down the hill." The family enters their red car, with Daddy Pig driving, Mummy Pig in front, and Peppa and George in the back, as a large yellow balloon with pink hearts floats beside them. As they drive along a road with green hills, the balloon floats away, surprising the family, and Daddy Pig exclaims, "Ah, let's catch that balloon!" The car speeds up with Peppa urging "Faster! Faster!" until Daddy Pig shouts "Stop!" The car screeches to a halt at a construction site where Mr. Bull, in a yellow hard hat and safety vest, stands by a traffic light and orange cone, looking concerned as the balloon drifts past, and Mr. Bull makes a 'moo' sound. At the construction site, Mr. Bull, holding a red stop sign, exclaims, 'Never a balloon! Better catch it!' as the yellow balloon with pink hearts floats past, observed by a concerned Peppa and George. The scene transitions to a vibrant playground where Daddy Pig, Mummy Pig, Peppa, George, Mr. Dog, the Zebra Character with Baby Zebras in a stroller, and Grandpa Rabbit are playing, and the balloon drifts in, causing surprise. Peppa shouts 'Sorry, coming through!' and 'Chasing balloon! Help!' while George pleads 'Please!' Daddy Pig instructs, 'Don't chase balloons!' and 'Catch the balloon!' Mr. Dog, holding balloons, adds, 'You heard the pig!' The group follows the balloon over a hospital entrance, where the White Rabbit Doctor remarks, 'Ah, just the pigs I was looking for,' and Mummy Pig exclaims, 'Oh, wait for me!' The balloon continues over a street with shops and a bridge, as the narrator states, 'Everyone chases the balloon all around town.' The Zebra Character questions, 'Uh? That's not one of my...' before the balloon lands near a swimming pool, where the group gathers in awe. In a bright, sunny park, Daddy Pig holds a large yellow balloon with pink hearts, which floats upward, catching the attention of Peppa, George, Mummy Pig, Mr. Bull, the Zebra Character with Baby Zebras, Grandpa Rabbit, and Mr. Dog. The Zebra Character is heard saying "running by the pole, please!" amidst running sounds, and the narrator states, "Until finally..." followed by Peppa exclaiming, "We got it!" Mummy Pig suggests, "Now, let's find out if it's a brother or sister, shall we?" Daddy Pig adds, "Before anything else goes wrong," and the group chants "Three, two, one!" The narrator reveals, "The balloon is empty," and "Now, no one knows what the new baby will be." Peppa reassures, "It's okay, as long as it's happy and nice, oh!" Mummy Pig agrees, and the Zebra Character apologizes, "Excuse me, sorry, Dr. Easel. Oh, I love your shoes," admitting, "I, uh, might have forgot to put the results in the balloon," but then declares, "But, I've got them here!" She humorously remarks, "Phew! That was a lot of running. Try doing it pregnant." Daddy Pig announces, "Well, little piggies, looks like you're getting..." which Mummy Pig finishes with, "A little sister!" The Zebra Character hands an envelope to Daddy Pig, who shares it with Mummy Pig, and the group stands together happily as the balloon floats gently above them.

Oh, I love your shoes," before admitting, "I, uh, might have forgot to put the results in the balloon." She then triumphantly declares, "But, I've got them here!" and, catching her breath, humorously remarks, "Phew! That was a lot of running. Try doing it pregnant." Daddy Pig's voice then announces, "Well, little piggies, looks like you're getting..." which Mummy Pig excitedly finishes with, "A little sister!" as the camera zooms in on Peppa and George, who are smiling and waving. The Zebra Character approaches with a small envelope, which she hands to Daddy Pig, who then shares it with Mummy Pig, both smiling warmly. The group stands together, happy and united, as the balloon floats gently above them, symbolizing joy and togetherness in the playful outdoor setting, punctuated by a final pig snort.

event_blocks.summary: In a sunny park, Peppa Pig, Daddy Pig, Mummy Pig, George Pig, Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, and Grandpa Rabbit gather for a surprise baby shower thrown by Mummy Sheep for Mummy Pig, with Peppa expressing excitement to meet the new baby. As the party begins with a spread of food, rain and thunder start, prompting Daddy Pig to lead everyone indoors to a kitchen. Despite the weather, Mummy Sheep quickly pivots, offering scones and announcing the party is ready, transitioning the celebration to a festive party room decorated with balloons, streamers, and a family drawing, where Peppa plays a drum and George a xylophone amidst cheerful music and party sounds. The scene then shifts to a brightly colored room with green walls and purple armchairs, where Mummy Sheep has moved the baby shower indoors due to the rain, featuring baby-themed games. A blue table displays baby animal toys arranged in diapers, and a purple table holds baby supplies like powder and bottles. Mummy Pig, Peppa Pig, and Madame Gazelle are joined by a Zebra character, two Baby Zebras, and another White Rabbit character. Peppa announces a nappy changing race, shouting "Ready, go!" as the group participates with animated reactions, with the narrator noting Mummy Pig's skill at changing nappies, all within a cheerful, lively environment filled with festive decorations and upbeat music.

raw_segments: The video opens with a cheerful, upbeat tune as Peppa Pig and her friends and family gather in a sunny park for a baby shower, with the narrator explaining, "Everyone is very excited to meet the new baby. But no one's more excited than Peppa." Peppa, in her red dress, excitedly waves, exclaiming, "I can't wait to meet you!" Daddy Pig, in his teal suit, and Mummy Pig, in her orange dress, smile warmly, while George, in his blue shirt, stands nearby. Other characters like Mr. Bull, Mr. Dog, and Grandpa Rabbit are present as a female voice, likely Mummy Sheep, announces, "Okay, places everyone. She'll be here any minute!" The narrator reveals, "Today, Mummy Sheep is throwing Mummy Pig a surprise party to celebrate her new baby," leading to a group cheer and the announcement, "It's a special party called a baby shower!" Mummy Pig gratefully responds, "Thank you so much!" as a lavish spread of cakes and snacks is shown. Suddenly, dark clouds appear, and the sound of rain and thunder begins, causing dismay among the guests. Daddy Pig urgently calls, "Everyone indoors!" as they rush to a brightly lit kitchen. The narrator comments, "Big wave for the mummy-to-be!" and Mummy Pig, sounding resigned, says, "Well, it was wonderful." The narrator adds, "Unfortunately, Mummy Sheep didn't plan for the British weather," and Peppa sighs, "Oh." Mummy Sheep reassures, "It's okay. We can just do it another day," before quickly changing her mind with "I know! Let's do it!" and offering "Uh, scone, anyone?" then announcing, "It's ready!" The scene shifts to a festive party room decorated with balloons, streamers, and a large drawing of the Pig family. Daddy Pig calls out "Peppa!" as the group celebrates joyfully with cheerful music and party sounds, Peppa playing a drum and George a xylophone, ensuring the celebration continues indoors with a lively atmosphere.

The scene opens in a brightly colored room with green walls and purple armchairs, adorned with children’s drawings of flowers, where Mummy Pig, in an orange dress, smiles warmly. A narrator's voice explains that Mummy Sheep has brought the baby shower indoors, away from the rain, and made some special baby-themed games. The camera then shifts to a blue table laden with playful baby animal toys—a purple cat, green frog, brown monkey, yellow tiger, and brown owl—all arranged in diapers, suggesting a baby-themed event. A close-up reveals a purple table with baby supplies, including jars of baby powder, bottles, and a bowl of red liquid, possibly baby food. Mummy Pig is then joined by Peppa Pig in a red dress and Madame Gazelle, a white rabbit in a pink dress, as they stand together with joyful expressions. A child's voice, distinctly Peppa's, exclaims "Wow!" and asks, "You did all this for me?", to which an adult female voice, likely Mummy Pig's, warmly replies, "Of course we did." The group expands to include a Zebra character in a red dress with glasses, two small Baby Zebras in purple dresses, and another White Rabbit character in a yellow dress, all gathered around the table of baby toys. Peppa Pig excitedly points at the toys, and her voice announces, "This is a nappy changing race. First to change three nappies wins!" She then enthusiastically shouts, "Ready, go!" as the group shares happy, animated reactions, indicating a celebratory baby shower atmosphere. The narrator adds that "Mummy Pig is very good at changing nappies." The room is filled with festive decorations, including balloons and colorful furniture, creating a cheerful and lively environment, all set to a backdrop of lighthearted, cheerful music and excited chatter.
